spec_match.rationale: The repaired page models the Chrome 139 Android background-freezing contract directly: it compares the previous 5-minute background budget with the new 1-minute budget, links the exact ChromeStatus feature, and keeps the Page Lifecycle reference visible.

interactive.rationale: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148.0.0.0: initial load rendered timelines, the workload select updated WebSocket, Service Worker, audio, and foreground explanations, and the backgrounded-at slider updated the live timing readout at 0s and 60s.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Boundary cases are now visible in the page output: 0s explains immediate backgrounding, 60s explains the late-background case, and the foreground workload renders active-only timelines with no warning or frozen segments.

references.rationale: The concept page now cites https://chromestatus.com/feature/5386725031149568 and the feature conformance page shows the same ID.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

summary: Repaired and re-reviewed with chrome-devtools-mcp. Before the fix, the inline script threw `Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected token ')'`, both controls lacked associated labels, and the ChromeStatus link pointed to the wrong feature. After the fix, the page has a clean console/network load, accessible workload and slider controls, correct ChromeStatus link, visible 0s/60s boundary copy, verified alternate workload branches, and the feature conformance route reports 6 pass / 0 fail.
